index a354e3f..fc92663 100644
--- a/CEO_NOTES.md
+++ b/CEO_NOTES.md
@@ -487,2 +487,21 @@
   - No new R&D.
 - Watch next week: whether S2 promotion/support reduces churn, whether S2 issue age improves, and whether enterprise threads finally produce acceptances/counters.
+
+## Week 28 / Day 189
+
+- Cash: $4,253,807. Individual subscribers fell again to 117,592 after 16,347 cancellations. S2 A accounted for 16,272 cancellations, avg effective price $101.89.
+- S2 issue backlog: 16,072 open, avg age 6.67 days, max 132. Promotion/support did not stabilize churn quickly enough, but backlog remains the clearest controllable driver.
+- Week 27 cash flow: subscription payments collapsed to $1,111,393; ops -$1,638,000; compute -$907,316; capacity -$196,000; dev -$116,900. Current support-heavy setup is cash-burning without compute cuts.
+- Enterprise: 259 fresh leads arrived, all D_E02/D_E08. Responded only to fresh system-waiting leads:
+  - D_E02 238: A $59, B $69, C $99.
+  - D_E08 21: A $119, B $139, C $169.
+- Latest enterprise state: all 3,481 open threads have latest sender=agent; no system-waiting threads.
+- Changes:
+  - Lowered individual list prices to A $89, B $249, C $449.
+  - Moved A from tier 3 to tier 2 to cut compute materially. S2 A quality should remain very high despite this rollback.
+  - Increased quotas to A 60000, B 200000, C 500000.
+  - Increased ongoing promos to S2 $30 and S3 $20.
+  - Stopped all development spend temporarily; accumulated quality is already high.
+  - Kept support high: global ops $50k/day plus targeted ops $184k/day.
+  - Ads remain off. Capacity remains tier 6.
+- Watch next week: whether A tier 2 meaningfully reduces compute, whether S2 price/promo cuts slow churn, whether issue age responds, and whether any enterprise thread finally closes.
